architecture.summary: PathFinder-PRM is a hierarchical, error-aware discriminative process reward model that classifies math and consistency errors at each intermediate step of LLM reasoning traces, then combines these fine-grained error signals to estimate step correctness. The system reads LLM-generated text (reasoning steps) and outputs both error classifications and a unified step-correctness reward score, enabling process-level steering of generation toward coherent solutions in mathematical problem solving.

biblio.title: Error Typing for Smarter Rewards: Improving Process Reward Models with Error-Aware Hierarchical Supervision

claims.statement: On PRMBench, PathFinder-PRM achieves a new state-of-the-art PRMScore of 67.7, outperforming the prior best (65.5)
PathFinder-PRM achieves state-of-the-art while using 3 less data
When applied to reward guided greedy search, our model yields prm@8 48.3, a +1.5 point gain over the strongest baseline
PathFinder-PRM first classifies math and consistency errors at each step, then combines these fine-grained signals to estimate step correctness
A 400K-sample dataset was constructed by enriching the human-annotated PRM800K corpus and RLHFlow Mistral traces with three-dimensional step-level labels

Decoupled error detection and reward estimation not only boost fine-grained error detection but also substantially improve end-to-end, reward-guided mathematical reasoning with greater data efficiency
